---
title: "Vector Basics"
documenttype: Handout
author:
  - name: Saaleh Poovathumkadavil
toc: true
abstract: "A practical R note for vectors, indexing, missing values, and small examples used by the statistics notes."

version: [v.1.0]
date: last-modified
execute:
  echo: fenced
  freeze: auto
bibliography: references.bib
bibliographystyle: "springer-humanities-author-date"
format:
  tufte-inspired-html: default
---


# Vectors

Vectors are the basic one-dimensional data structure in R. They are useful for
small probability examples and regression inputs.

```{r echo=TRUE}
x <- c(2, 3, 5, 7)
x[x > 3]
```

Use this with [Linear Models](../../../statistics/regression/linear-models/index.html) and
[Random Variables](../../../statistics/probability/random-variables/index.html).

# Missing Values

Missing values are represented with `NA`.

```{r echo=TRUE}
y <- c(1, NA, 4)
y[!is.na(y)]
```
